response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled polyglot individual contributor with strong distributed-systems experience and a brief recent foray into agentic AI tooling at Google, but he presents no evidence of engineering leadership, team scaling, executive communication, or architectural ownership at any level — all of which are hard requirements for this CTO role. His fintech exposure is limited to one developer position at RIDE Capital, his AI work appears to have begun approximately one month ago, and his career trajectory is entirely that of a senior/staff IC rather than a technical leader. The gap between his demonstrated capabilities and the role's requirements is fundamental, not a matter of positioning.
strengths: Broad backend and distributed-systems experience including low-latency microservices handling millions of RPS at Sociomantic and event-driven architecture across multiple stacks
Early hands-on exposure to agentic AI infrastructure and MCP tooling at Google, indicating relevance to the domain even if very recent
Consistent track record of working across full-stack, embedded, and platform contexts, demonstrating adaptability and technical range
concerns: Zero demonstrated engineering leadership or people management — no evidence of managing, mentoring, or scaling a team at any point in a 20-year career
AI and agentic-systems experience is limited to roughly one month at Google as a contributor, far below the production depth and architectural ownership the role requires
No executive-level experience: no board communication, no roadmap ownership, no governance design, no cross-functional or organizational accountability
